summary: Ukrainian forces destroyed a Russian UAV‑operator training centre in Snizhne, killing 65 trainees, while Russian tactical aviation intensified guided‑bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk. Shahed missile attacks surged across central Ukraine, and a Ukrainian drone strike ignited a massive fire at the Sizran oil refinery deep inside Russia. Both sides report heavy UAV losses and continued civilian casualties, indicating a highly active and volatile front.
key_points: Ukrainian drones destroyed the Russian UAV‑operator training centre in Snizhne, Donetsk, killing 65 personnel (Source: @UkraineNow/81356).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/62347).
Shahed missiles were launched from Sumy to Poltava, from Poltava to Cherkasy and Chernihiv, and from Chernihiv to Kyiv region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66879).
Additional Shahed attacks hit Kriviy Rih, Zhytomyr‑Khmelnytsky, and Kropyvnytskyi‑Mykolaiv directions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66852).
Ukrainian special‑operations strike set fire to the Sizran oil refinery 800 km inside Russia, disrupting fuel supplies (Source: @UkraineNow/81362).
Russian defence ministry reported shooting down 167 Ukrainian UAVs and striking Krivoy Rog, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, Kramatorsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
A Russian missile hit Dnipro, injuring a 58‑year‑old woman and damaging multiple apartments (Source: @UkraineNow/81358).
UAVs were observed heading to Kryvyi Rih, Dnipro, Sumy, Mykolaiv, Nikopol, Pavlohrad and Kamianske from various directions (Source: @kpszsu/62344).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces eliminated a Russian UAV‑operator training centre in Snizhne, killing 65 trainees. Russian tactical aviation escalated guided‑bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk while Shahed missile attacks proliferated across central Ukraine. Both sides report heavy UAV losses and civilian casualties, underscoring a highly active conflict.

points: Ukrainian special‑operations units used 11 strike drones carrying 100 kg bombs to destroy the Russian UAV‑training centre in Snizhne, Donetsk, killing 65 operators and the centre commander "Bury" (Source: @UkraineNow/81356).

A Ukrainian drone strike ignited a massive fire at the Sizran oil refinery, a 9 million‑ton capacity plant 800 km from the Ukrainian border, disrupting fuel supplies for Russian aviation and ground forces (Source: @UkraineNow/81362).

Ukrainian forces reported multiple Shahed missile launches from Sumy to Poltava, from Poltava to Cherkasy and Chernihiv, and from Chernihiv to Kyiv region, indicating coordinated long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66879).

Ukrainian UAV activity was noted heading toward Kryvyi Rih, Dnipro, Sumy, Mykolaiv, Nikopol, Pavlohrad and Kamianske, suggesting reconnaissance and strike missions across the eastern and southern fronts (Source: @kpszsu/62344).

Ukrainian intelligence indicated that Russian losses have risen to 254 personnel per km² of advance in February 2026, reflecting attrition from intensified air‑defence and ground operations (Source: @DeepStateUA/23527).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on targets in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, marking a shift to precision strike tactics on Ukrainian rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/62347).
The Russian defence ministry claimed 167 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down on 20 May, while conducting strikes on Krivoy Rog, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy and Kramatorsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).

Russian forces used Shahed missiles from Zhytomyr towards Khmelnytskyi (Shepetivka district) and from Kropyvnytskyi towards Mykolaiv (Bashkansky district), expanding the missile corridor south‑west (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66852).

In the Belgorod region, Ukrainian FPV drones struck civilian cars in Striuguni and Golovino, injuring civilians and causing mine‑explosive injuries, prompting Russian retaliatory strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Russian special‑operations reported increased activity of tactical aviation on the south‑east axis, warning of potential air‑strike threats to front‑line oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/62336).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed approaching Kryvyi Rih from the east and Dnipro from the north, indicating pressure on the eastern industrial corridor (Source: @kpszsu/62344).
Russian strikes hit the villages of Bely Kolodez, Izbitskoe, Ryasnoe and Staritsa in Kharkiv region, intensifying combat on the eastern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Ukrainian forces reported drone attacks on the village of Suzemka in Bryansk region, injuring a driver of the APK "Miratorg", showing cross‑border UAV activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Heavy battles continued near Krasnaya Liman and around the villages of Ilninovka and Novodmitrovka, with Russian forces attempting to expand control zones (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Shahed missiles launched from Sumy to Poltava and from Poltava to Chernihiv demonstrate a north‑to‑central missile corridor threatening northern oblasts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66879).
Ukrainian UAVs were sighted heading to Sumy from the north, suggesting reconnaissance over the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/62344).
Russian tactical aviation warned of increased aerial threat on the south‑east direction, potentially affecting northern border regions (Source: @kpszsu/62336).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed missile from Chernihiv to Kyiv region (Boryspil district), highlighting vulnerability of the capital's northern suburbs (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66879).
Ukrainian drones struck the Sizran oil refinery deep in southern Russia, affecting fuel supplies for Russian forces in the south (Source: @UkraineNow/81362).
Russian guided‑bomb sorties targeted Zaporizhzhia oblast, intensifying pressure on Ukrainian southern defensive lines (Source: @kpszsu/62347).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed heading to Mykolaiv from the east and to the outskirts of Mykolaiv city, indicating offensive operations in the south (Source: @kpszsu/62346).
Russian strikes on the Kherson region injured a civilian in Aleshki, showing continued combat in the southern theater (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Russian defence ministry reported shooting down 167 Ukrainian UAVs on 20 May, reflecting intense air‑defence activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
Ukrainian forces claimed a 2‑fold increase in Shahed interceptions, with a 95 % success rate against aerial targets (Source: @DeepStateUA/23527).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, marking a shift to precision air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/62347).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted multiple cross‑border attacks in Bryansk and Belgorod regions, demonstrating extended air‑operational reach (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones eliminated a Russian UAV‑operator training centre in Snizhne, Donetsk, killing 65 trainees and the centre commander (Source: @UkraineNow/81356).
Russian tactical aviation employed guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, increasing precision strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62347).
Shahed missile launches from Sumy, Poltava and Chernihiv created multiple strike corridors across central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66879).
Ukrainian special‑operations strike set fire to the Sizran oil refinery 800 km inside Russia, disrupting fuel supplies (Source: @UkraineNow/81362).
Russian defence ministry reported downing 167 Ukrainian UAVs while striking five major Ukrainian cities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30330).
A Russian missile hit Dnipro, injuring a civilian and damaging several apartment blocks (Source: @UkraineNow/81358).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed heading toward Kryvyi Rih, Dnipro, Sumy, Mykolaiv, Nikopol, Pavlohrad and Kamianske, indicating broad reconnaissance and strike activity (Source: @kpszsu/62344).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are escalating precision air strikes and guided‑bomb deployments, particularly in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, while expanding Shahed missile corridors from the north. Ukraine counters with high‑intensity UAV operations, striking deep targets such as the Sizran refinery and disrupting Russian logistics. Both sides suffer significant UAV losses, indicating a contested air domain that will shape upcoming ground operations.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are monitoring increased UAV activity and have urged tighter target selection after a Ukrainian drone entered Estonian airspace, reflecting heightened NATO vigilance. Ukraine continues to bolster its air‑defence and drone production, while Russia leverages tactical aviation to pressure southern and eastern fronts. Regional actors remain on alert for spill‑over effects, especially along the northern corridors.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source reports confirm a surge in Shahed missile launches and a notable rise in Ukrainian UAV strike range, suggesting improved operational planning. The destruction of the Russian UAV training centre may degrade Russian drone capabilities in the short term. Continued civilian casualties in Dnipro and Kherson underscore the conflict's humanitarian impact and the need for reinforced civil defence measures.
